mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio above 0.5 for long positions during these periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising corporate output, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the predictive IC turns negative in more than 50% of rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer price inflation erodes real household purchasing power and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal shows no predictive power in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed cases, invalidating the growth signal.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below its recent trend signals a sudden supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 70% of instances when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month average, or the signal loses predictive accuracy in out-of-sample tests.
